source_text: Margins: 1 inch all around. On letterhead, typing starts more than 1 inch from the top. Footers are 0.5 inch from the bottom of the page. Do not right, center, or full justify text or use proportional spacing.
plain_language_rule: Standard letters must have 1-inch margins on all sides (top, bottom, left, right). On letterhead paper, the first line of typing must start more than 1 inch from the top edge. Page numbers and footers on continuation pages must be positioned 0.5 inch from the bottom of the page. Text must not be right-justified, center-justified, or fully justified. Proportional spacing must not be used. Exception: directive header/footer formatting may be retained if included in the source text.
renderer_impact: Page geometry configuration in ReportLab canvas; determines BOUNDARY_SPACINGS for top margin (72 pt = 1 inch), bottom margin (36 pt = 0.5 inch for footers), and left/right margins (72 pt = 1 inch each); letterhead rendering must account for typing start position below 1-inch threshold; text must use left justification only with monospaced or fixed-pitch spacing
notes: Margin rules are measurable renderer/layout requirements. Top margin: 1 inch (72 pt); Bottom margin for footers/page numbers: 0.5 inch (36 pt); Left/Right margins: 1 inch (72 pt) each. Letterhead typing start must exceed 1 inch from top edge. Text justification must be left-aligned only (no right, center, or full justify). Proportional spacing is forbidden. These are deterministic layout constraints, not advisory guidance.
